file	README.md
dir	lib
file	mix.exs
dir	test

# Invoice Pipeline

Input rows are pipe-delimited as:

    customer|category|unit_price|quantity

Ignore blank rows and malformed rows. Group valid rows by customer.
Customers and category labels should render in alphabetical order.

For each customer:

- subtotal is the sum of unit_price * quantity.
- discount is 10% of subtotal when subtotal is at least 100.00.
- discount is a flat 5.00 when subtotal is at least 50.00 and below 100.00.
- discount is 0.00 otherwise.
- tax is computed from category totals before discount:
  food: 2%, book: 0%, tool: 7.5%, service: 9%, unknown categories: 0%.
- total is subtotal - discount + tax.
- render all money values with exactly two decimal places.

The public API is `InvoicePipeline.run/1`, implemented through the
`InvoicePipeline.Parser`, `InvoicePipeline.Rules`, and
`InvoicePipeline.Renderer` modules.

defmodule InvoicePipeline do
  @moduledoc false

  def run(source) do
    source
    |> InvoicePipeline.Parser.parse()
    |> InvoicePipeline.Rules.summarize()
    |> InvoicePipeline.Renderer.render()
  end
end

defmodule InvoicePipeline.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(_source), do: []
end

defmodule InvoicePipeline.Rules do
  @moduledoc false

  def summarize(_rows), do: []
end

defmodule InvoicePipeline.Renderer do
  @moduledoc false

  def render(_summaries), do: []
end

defmodule InvoicePipelineTest do
  use ExUnit.Case, async: true

  alias InvoicePipeline.Parser
  alias InvoicePipeline.Rules
  alias InvoicePipeline.Renderer

  test "parser keeps valid rows and skips malformed input" do
    source = "ACME|food|12.00|2\nBROKEN|food|oops|3\n\nZED|tool|40.00|1\nSKIP|too|few\n"

    assert Parser.parse(source) == [
             %{customer: "ACME", category: "food", unit_price: 12.0, quantity: 2},
             %{customer: "ZED", category: "tool", unit_price: 40.0, quantity: 1}
           ]
  end

  test "rules compute discounts, taxes, totals, and category totals" do
    rows = [
      %{customer: "MIRA", category: "service", unit_price: 100.0, quantity: 1},
      %{customer: "BETA", category: "food", unit_price: 2.5, quantity: 4},
      %{customer: "MIRA", category: "service", unit_price: 25.0, quantity: 2}
    ]

    assert Rules.summarize(rows) == [
             %{
               customer: "BETA",
               subtotal: 10.0,
               discount:...

args.new_string:
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(source) do
    source
    |> String.split("\n")
    |> Enum.map(&String.trim/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&(&1 == ""))
    |> Enum.map(&parse_line/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&is_nil/1)
  end

  defp parse_line(line) do
    parts = String.split(line, "|")

    with [customer, category, unit_price_str, quantity_str] <- parts,
         {unit_price, ""} <- Float.parse(unit_price_str),
         {quantity, ""} <- Integer.parse(quantity_str) do
      %{
        customer: customer,
        category: category,
        unit_price: unit_price,
        quantity: quantity
      }
    else
      _ -> nil
    end
  end
end

end

defmodule InvoicePipeline.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(source) do
    source
    |> String.split("\n")
    |> Enum.map(&String.trim/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&(&1 == ""))
    |> Enum.map(&parse_line/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&is_nil/1)
  end

  defp parse_line(line) do
    parts = String.split(line, "|")

    with [customer, category, unit_price_str, quantity_str] <- parts,
         {unit_price, ""} <- Float.parse(unit_price_str),
         {quantity, ""} <- Integer.parse(quantity_str) do
      %{
        customer: customer,
        category: category,
        unit_price: unit_price,
        quantity: quantity
      }
    else
      _ -> nil
    end
  end
end
args.old_string:
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(_source), do: []
end

args.new_string:
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Rules do
  @moduledoc false

  @tax_rates %{
    "food" => 0.02,
    "book" => 0.0,
    "tool" => 0.075,
    "service" => 0.09
  }

  def summarize(rows) do
    rows
    |> Enum.group_by(& &1.customer)
    |> Enum.map(fn {customer, customer_rows} ->
      customer_summary(customer, customer_rows)
    end)
    |> Enum.sort_by(& &1.customer)
  end

  defp customer_summary(customer, rows) do
    category_totals =
      rows
      |> Enum.group_by(& &1.category)
      |> Enum.map(fn {category, cat_rows} ->
        total = Enum.reduce(cat_rows, 0.0, fn row, acc ->
          acc + row.unit_price * row.quantity
        end)
        {category, total}
      end)
      |> Map.new()

    subtotal = Enum.reduce(category_totals, 0.0, fn {_cat, total}, acc -> acc + total end)

    tax =
      category_totals
      |> Enum.reduce(0.0, fn {category, total}, acc ->
        rate = Map.get(@tax_rates, category, 0.0)
        acc + total * rate
      end)

    discount = calculate_discount(subtotal)
    total = subtotal - discount + tax

    %{
      customer: customer,
      subtotal: subtotal,
      discount: discount,
      tax: tax,
      total: total,
      categories: category_totals
    }
  end

  defp calculate_discount(subtotal) when subtotal >= 100.0 do
    subtotal * 0.10
  end

  defp calculate_discount(subtotal) when subtotal >= 50.0 do
    5.0
  end

  defp calculate_discount(_subtotal) do
    0.0
  end
end
args.old_string:
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Rules do
  @moduledoc false

  def summarize(_rows), do: []
end

args.new_string:
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Renderer do
  @moduledoc false

  def render(summaries) do
    summaries
    |> Enum.sort_by(& &1.customer)
    |> Enum.map(&render_summary/1)
  end

  defp render_summary(summary) do
    categories_str =
      summary.categories
      |> Enum.sort_by(fn {cat, _total} -> cat end)
      |> Enum.map(fn {cat, total} -> "#{cat}:#{format_money(total)}" end)
      |> Enum.join(",")

    "customer=#{summary.customer} " <>
    "subtotal=#{format_money(summary.subtotal)} " <>
    "discount=#{format_money(summary.discount)} " <>
    "tax=#{format_money(summary.tax)} " <>
    "total=#{format_money(summary.total)} " <>
    "categories=#{categories_str}"
  end

  defp format_money(value) do
    :io_lib.format("~.2f", [value])
    |> to_string()
  end
end
args.old_string:
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Renderer do
  @moduledoc false

  def render(_summaries), do: []
end

cat > /workspace/lib/invoice_pipeline/parser.ex << 'EOF'
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(source) do
    source
    |> String.split("\n")
    |> Enum.map(&String.trim/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&(&1 == ""))
    |> Enum.map(&parse_line/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&is_nil/1)
  end

  defp parse_line(line) do
    parts = String.split(line, "|")

    with [customer, category, unit_price_str, quantity_str] <- parts,
         {unit_price, ""} <- Float.parse(unit_price_str),
         {quantity, ""} <- Integer.parse(quantity_str) do
      %{
        customer: customer,
        category: category,
        unit_price: unit_price,
        quantity: quantity

    else
      _ -> nil

end
EOF

cat > /workspace/lib/invoice_pipeline/rules.ex << 'EOF'
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Rules do
  @moduledoc false

  @tax_rates %{
    "food" => 0.02,
    "book" => 0.0,
    "tool" => 0.075,
    "service" => 0.09
  }

  def summarize(rows) do
    rows
    |> Enum.group_by(& &1.customer)
    |> Enum.map(fn {customer, customer_rows} ->
      customer_summary(customer, customer_rows)
    end)
    |> Enum.sort_by(& &1.customer)
  end

  defp customer_summary(customer, rows) do
    category_totals =
      rows
      |> Enum.group_by(& &1.category)
      |> Enum.map(fn {category, cat_rows} ->
        total = Enum.reduce(cat_rows, 0.0, fn row, acc ->
          acc + row.unit_price * row.quantity
        end)
        {category, total}
      end)
      |> Map.new()

    subtotal = Enum.reduce(category_totals, 0.0, fn {_cat, total}, acc -> acc + total end)

    tax =
      category_totals
      |> Enum.reduce(0.0, fn {category, total}, acc ->
        rate = Map.get(@tax_rates, category, 0.0)
        acc + total * rate
      end)

    discount = calculate_discount(subtotal)
    total = subtotal - discount + tax

    %{
      customer: customer,
      subtotal: subtotal,
      discount: discount,
      tax: tax,
      total: total,
      categories: category_totals
    }
  end

  defp calculate_discount(subtotal) when subtotal >= 100.0 do
    subtotal * 0.10
  end

  defp calculate_discount(subtotal) when subtotal >= 50.0 do
    5.0
  end

  defp calculate_discount(_subtotal) do
    0.0
  end
end
EOF

cat > /workspace/lib/invoice_pipeline/renderer.ex << 'EOF'
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Renderer do
  @moduledoc false

  def render(summaries) do

  defp render_summary(summary) do

    "customer=#{summary.customer} " <>

    "tax=#{format_money(summary.tax)} " <>

  end

  defp format_money(value) do

    |> to_string()
  end
end
EOF
